name: Dum Dum Park Bharat Chakra
description:
The concept for 2025 Durga Puja was amazing, it was based on unseen cosmic energy. Entire pandal was given a quite mysterious and dark feel, with spiral lighting. Even Maa's idol was more to merge with a calming energy.

Crowd Management was superb. Pandal was wheelchair friendly, and people were giving easier access to wheelchair.

name: Hatibagan Sarbojonin
description:
Theme - Athaha Ghat Katha (অথঃ ঘাট কথা)

Athaha Ghat Kotha celebrates Kolkatas forgotten ghats, once vibrant epicenters of civic, cultural, and spiritual life along the Hooghly. From theatre rehearsals, wrestling matches, and political gatherings to the immersion of clay idols and sacred rituals like abapatrika bathing, these ghats bore witness to love, farewells, and devotion. Now largely absent from public memory, they remain silent sentinels of Bengal's history and traditions. This Durga Puja presentation evokes the untold stories and layered histories embedded in their steps, inviting visitors to experience the poetic rhythm of the river, heritage, and ritual, where the ghatsthemselves become portalsto forgotten naratives.

name: Kumartuli Park
description:
Kumartuli Park Durgotsab is one of the most renowned and artistically distinguished pujas in North Kolkata. Situated in the cultural heart of Kumartuli — the hub of Bengal’s idol makers — this puja beautifully captures the essence of art, devotion, and heritage.

Each year, the club presents breathtaking themes that seamlessly combine tradition with modern creativity. The pandal design, lighting arrangements, and Durga idol are always crafted with exceptional detail and aesthetic appeal, reflecting the unmatched skill of local artisans.

The ambience is vibrant yet well-organized, with efficient crowd management and safety arrangements ensuring a comfortable experience for visitors. The cultural programs and festive atmosphere make it a highlight of the Durga Puja season.

Overall, Kumartuli Park Durgotsab continues to set high standards of creativity and devotion, making it a must-visit destination during Kolkata’s grand Durga Puja celebrations.

name: Kashi Bose Lane Durga Puja Committee
description:
An Artistic Triumph in the Heart of North Kolkata
Kashi Bose Lane Durga Puja Committee has once again proven why it remains an essential destination for pandal hoppers. This year's celebration is a masterful blend of profound artistic vision and deep-rooted tradition, creating an experience that is both visually stunning and emotionally resonant.
Nestled in the charming lanes of North Kolkata, the pandal transforms the entire locality into a living art installation. The theme is executed with exceptional creativity and an incredible eye for detail, utilizing unique materials and concepts that captivate from the very first glance. It’s a thoughtful and immersive experience that encourages you to pause and appreciate the craftsmanship.
The pratima (idol) is the heart of the setup, perfectly harmonizing with the overarching theme. The artistry is simply breathtaking, conveying a powerful sense of divinity while fitting seamlessly into the conceptual narrative. The lighting and sound design further elevate the atmosphere, creating a truly magical and memorable environment, particularly in the evening.
Despite being one of the most popular pujas in the area, the crowd is managed with remarkable efficiency by the organizers and local volunteers. The queues are orderly, and the entire process feels smooth and respectful, allowing everyone to enjoy the artistry without feeling overwhelmed.
In summary, Kashi Bose Lane is a must-visit for anyone who appreciates the creative spirit of Durga Puja. It is a brilliant showcase of community, culture, and artistic excellence. Highly recommended!

name: College Square Durga Puja
description:
I recently visited College Square Durga Puja, and it was an amazing experience for me. The entire area was beautifully decorated, and the energy during the festival was incredible. I really enjoyed walking around and seeing the stunning idol of Goddess Durga along with the elaborate decorations and lights. The crowd was lively, full of people celebrating with joy and enthusiasm, yet the atmosphere felt very festive and welcoming.

I also loved the cultural programs happening around — it added so much vibrancy to the visit. The food stalls were tempting, offering a variety of local treats, and it was fun to try some while soaking in the festive vibe. Overall, spending time at College Square during Durga Puja made me feel very connected to the celebration, and it was one of the most memorable Durga Puja experiences I’ve had in Kolkata.

name: Santosh Mitra Square
description: Santosh Mitra Square Durga Puja is one of the most prominent and visited community pujas in Central Kolkata, renowned for its extravagant and innovative themes. Each year, the organizers create breathtaking pandals that often replicate famous global monuments or intricate designs using unconventional materials. The puja is particularly famous for its stunning lighting displays and the majestic idol of Goddess Durga, attracting millions of devotees and tourists. It remains a major highlight of the city's cultural calendar, consistently pushing the boundaries of artistic creativity during the festival.

name: Ultadanga Pallyshree Durga Puja
description: Ultadanga Pallyshree Durga Puja is a remarkable celebration that captures the essence of community spirit and artistic expression. Known for its impressive themes and stunning decorations, this pandal showcases a blend of traditional and modern designs that leave a lasting impression on visitors. The idol is intricately crafted, embodying both devotion and creativity. The atmosphere during the festivities is lively yet inviting, making it a perfect spot for families and friends to gather and celebrate together. Ultadanga Pallyshree is a must-visit for anyone seeking to enjoy a beautifully curated and culturally enriching Durga Puja experience.

name: Beliaghata 33 No. Palli Basi Brinda
description:
Beliaghata 33 Pally Durga Puja is known for its creative and meaningful theme-based pandals.

I have been visiting rhis pandal since they dis a taxi based theme.

Every year, the club presents thoughtful concepts with strong artistic execution, making it a notable stop for pandal hoppers in Kolkata.

Other than Durga Puja, their Saraswati puja is also very nice

name: Sandhani Club Puja Mandap
description: Beleghata Sandhani Club truly delivers one of the most memorable Durga Pujas in the city. The pandal design is always innovative and beautifully executed, with great attention to detail that makes it stand out from the crowd. The idol of Maa Durga is majestic and divine, radiating positive energy the moment you step inside.

name: Ekdalia Evergreen Club
description:
Pandal here in 2025 resembles the Gopuram of South Indian temples. Other than Pandal the decoration outside was marvelous. There were beautiful light statues.

Though it was raining, but there was significant crowd, but crowd management was awesome. This was one of the large pandals I visited

name: Singhi Park Sarbojanin Durga Puja Committee
description:
Singhi Park Sarbojanin Durga Puja is truly one of the crown jewels of South Kolkata’s Durga Puja celebrations. This year’s pandal was a perfect blend of creativity, devotion, and grandeur. The entrance itself was spectacular, designed with a massive themed structure and vibrant illuminations that immediately set the festive mood. The lighting arrangements along the lane leading to the pandal were mesmerizing – from the peacock-themed installation to the circus-inspired illuminated artworks, every corner was glowing with life and color.

As you step inside, the magnificence of the idol takes your breath away. Maa Durga, with her children, looked divine against the beautifully decorated backdrop. The craftsmanship of the pandal – from intricate wall patterns to the chandeliers adorning the ceiling – reflected both artistic excellence and cultural heritage. The organizers did a commendable job handling the huge crowd smoothly, ensuring that everyone could get a good darshan without too much waiting.

What makes Singhi Park special every year is its ability to combine tradition with innovation. The spiritual atmosphere, cultural richness, and stunning visual storytelling make it a must-visit during Durga Puja. Whether you’re a local or a visitor, this pandal is an unforgettable experience and truly upholds the grandeur of Bengal’s biggest festival.

name: Hindusthan Park Sarbojanin Durgotsab Committee
description:
One of the most underrated Durga Puja of South Calcutta...
Even if the pandal was not about a topic of discussion, their idol surely was and continues to be ...
Even this year they managed to pull out their creativity in every way possible.
Although I visited on the day before inaugural, I somehow managed to have a peek-a-boo on their artistry❤️

name: Tridhara Sammilani
description:
The 'Cholo Phiri' theme was a nostalgic masterpiece, beautifully capturing the essence of our roots and tradition."

"I was mesmerized by the 'Dhwani' installation; the blend of traditional brass work with sensor-driven light and sound was truly innovative."

"The Black-themed pandal and the live Shiv Tandav performance created a divine and powerful atmosphere that I’ll never forget."

"Tridhara remains one of the best spots in South Kolkata for its unique artistic vision
#Durgapuja2025 #kolkatadairies #maa

name: Deshapriya Park
description: Deshapriya Park has truly outdone itself this year. The towering temple pandal is a masterpiece of architecture and the intricate lighting design is a visual feast at night. Inside, the traditional Durga protima is beautiful, highlighted by the magnificent chandeliers. A must-see spectacle for the 2025 Pujo season!

name: Maddox Square Park
description:
Walking into Maddox Square, the first thing that hits me is the electric atmosphere. Between the sweeping spotlights of the 'City of Joy' stage and the cool winter breeze, the park felt completely transformed into a festive wonderland.📷
It was vibrant and full of life ,families, friends, and music lovers all coming together under the open sky.👀

Seeing the mini-rides and the giant wheel lit up brought back so much nostalgia. It’s that perfect mix of a modern music festival and a traditional Kolkata 'mela'.🥳🎉

One of the highlights was the curated collection of small businesses. I spent a good amount of time browsing through the boutique stalls. It was the perfect place to pick up unique, handcrafted items that you just can't find in big malls.

name: Bhowanipur 75 Palli
description:
Bhowanipore 75 Palli: A Stunning Tribute to "Binodini"!
Bhowanipore 75 Palli has created an absolutely awesome Durga Puja pandal this year. Their innovative and beautiful theme, "Binodini," is a spectacular tribute, showcasing remarkable and unique ideas. The pandal is stunning, and the idol is equally beautiful in its own way. This innovative display has created a fantastic atmosphere, and it’s a must-visit for all pandal-hoppers. Be sure to go ASAP to avoid the inevitable crowds!

name: Chetla Agrani Durga Puja Pandal
description:
Chetla Agrani’s Durga Puja pandal is truly a visual treat. Every year they bring something unique, and this time the theme, lighting, and decoration were absolutely stunning. The detailing in the idol and the beautiful ambiance create a perfect festive vibe. Crowd management is also good, so you can enjoy comfortably.❣️

Overall, a must-visit pandal in Kolkata for its creativity, cultural feel, and smooth experience. Highly recommended for pandal-hoppers!🙏🏻♥️🙏🏻

name: Suruchi Sangha
description:
I had the privilege of visiting the Suruchi Sangha Puja Pandal this year, and I must say it was one of the most thought-provoking and moving experiences of Durga Puja. The theme revolved around the struggles and sacrifices of our freedom fighters during India’s fight for independence, and the organizers have truly brought history alive through their creativity.

The moment I stepped inside, I felt transported back to the pages of history. The pandal beautifully showcased various episodes of our independence movement — from the salt satyagraha to revolutionary struggles, from the voices of leaders like Netaji, Gandhi, and Bhagat Singh to the countless unsung heroes who gave their lives for the country. The artistry, detailing, and storytelling through installations were exceptional, creating a deep sense of respect and pride for our nation.

What impressed me most was how the pandal did not just focus on grand leaders but also highlighted the ordinary men and women whose courage and determination contributed equally to our freedom. The use of sound, light, and symbolic art pieces gave a powerful emotional touch, making visitors reflect on the value of independence that we often take for granted today.

Suruchi Sangha deserves immense appreciation for using Durga Puja — a festival of joy and devotion — as a platform to remind us of our roots, our history, and the price of freedom. This pandal is not just a visual treat but also an inspiring lesson in patriotism. Truly, it stands as one of the best pandals of this year.

name: Mudiali Club
description:
My visit to Mudiali Club Durga Pujo 2025 was nothing short of extraordinary. This year’s theme, “Prakritir Pratyabartan” (Return of Nature), instantly drew me in with its calm, earthy aesthetic.

The moment I entered, I felt surrounded by the soothing presence of nature—bamboo structures, terracotta elements, flowing water motifs, and soft ambient sounds that recreated a serene forest environment right in the heart of Kolkata.

What truly amazed me was the pandal’s eco-friendly design. Every element, from the textures to the lighting, was crafted using sustainable materials, making the theme feel authentic and meaningful. The **idol of Maa Durga** was breathtaking, adorned in natural tones that perfectly matched the concept of environmental harmony.

Despite the huge crowd, Mudiali Club managed everything smoothly, making my entire visit peaceful and memorable. This pandal wasn’t just visually stunning—it carried a message that stayed with me long after I left.

name: 95 Pally Association
description:
I recently visited 95 Pally Association Durga Puja, and it was a really lovely experience for me. Right when I stepped inside, I was greeted by beautiful decorations and a warm, festive vibe that made everything feel joyful and inviting. The idol of Goddess Durga was crafted with great attention to detail, and the way it was displayed gave the whole pandal a very traditional and respectful feel.

The atmosphere around was full of celebration — with dhak beats, lights, and people from the community enjoying the festival together. I enjoyed walking around and seeing all the creative elements the organizers had put into the decorations. Even though there were quite a lot of people, the energy felt welcoming rather than chaotic.

There were also small food stalls and snacks nearby, which added to the fun of the visit. Overall, visiting 95 Pally Association Durga Puja made me feel really connected to the festive spirit, and it was one of the enjoyable and memorable Durga Puja visits I’ve had in Kolkata.

name: Ballygunge 21 Pally
description: Ballygunge is a old para of South Kolkata. There are a few large pujas that happen here without a few 100 meters of each other. 21 pally has been a long established Puja and if you are in South Kolkata this should be added to your list so you must visit. The Puja is beautiful and the idols are amazing.

name: Rajdanga Naba Uday Sangha
description:
A Beacon of Creativity and Community
Rajdanga Naba Uday Sangha is a true gem in Kolkata’s cultural landscape, particularly renowned for its exceptional Durga Puja celebrations. It’s more than just a local festival; it's an annual showcase of profound creativity and social consciousness.
What consistently sets this club apart is its ability to weave deep, often thought-provoking themes into its pandal and idol design, moving beyond mere spectacle to deliver a powerful message. From highlighting the plight of refugees with the theme 'Address' to exploring introspection with 'Proshno' ("The Question"), the organizers demonstrate an impressive commitment to conceptual artistry.
The sheer scale, intricate craftsmanship, and innovative use of materials in their decorations are a testament to the talent involved. Visitors are not only enthralled by the beautiful, unique idol of Maa Durga but also by the entire immersive experience. The club beautifully merges traditional devotion with modern, engaging conceptual art, making the visit a truly memorable one.
With its excellent location near Acropolis Mall and a strong emphasis on community engagement, Rajdanga Naba Uday Sangha delivers a well-rounded and deeply enriching cultural experience every year. It’s a definite must-visit for anyone seeking a Puja that is both visually stunning and intellectually stimulating.

name: Behala Nutan Dal
description:
⭐ Review for Behala Nutan Dal Durga Puja ⭐🙏🏻

This year’s grand theme is “Shibani Dham” 🛕✨ – recreating the majestic Rajprasad inspired by Bundelkhand’s royal heritage and devotion to Lord Shiva. The story of Raja Rajkishore and Rai Kumari comes alive through this magnificent pandal, blending history, art, faith, and culture beautifully.

From the moment you walk through the mesmerising passageway, covered in intricate murals and glowing lights, you feel transported into a palace courtyard. The pandal’s detailing is breathtaking – every wall, arch, and carving has been recreated with perfection 🛕🌸.

At the centre stands a divine Lord Shiva statue 🕉️🛕, brought to life with magical lighting effects – calm, powerful, and spiritually uplifting. The idol of Maa Durga 🪔🙏🐅 radiates elegance, with her lions 🐅🐅 adding to the aura of strength and protection. The entire setup creates a serene yet majestic ambience that leaves you awestruck.

🌟 On their 60th year, Behala Nutan Dal has truly delivered a masterpiece – one of the most divine and artistically stunning pandals of Durga Puja 2025. A must-visit for every pandal hopper!

#DurgaPuja2025 #BehalaNutanDal #ShibaniDham #KolkataPuja

name: Behala Buroshibtala Janakalyan Sangha
description: One more outstanding work by Kolkata South. Surochi Sangha is the closest landmark when traveling towards Behala. The tiny space always manages to create a remarkable theme that will leave you in awe.

name: Barisha Sarbojonin
description: Barisha Sarbojonin is well known for their Durga Puja presentation / theme. Also there is another well known festival celebrated, Chandi  Mata Puja. Its celebration is as big and good as Durga Puja. Huge Mela is setup at the time of this puja with different types of items being sold.

name: Ajeya Sanghati Club
description: Ajeya Sanghati Club’s 2025 Durga Puja theme “Sarbongsaha” beautifully captures the spirit of unity and inclusiveness. The pandal design is detailed and immersive, combining modern creativity with traditional reverence. The idol stands out with its graceful presence, drawing visitors into a space that feels both divine and welcoming. A thoughtful and artistic celebration - definitely worth a visit.

name: Naktala Udayan Sangha
description:
I recently visited Naktala Udayan Sangha during Durga Puja, and it was an absolutely grand and memorable experience for me. From the moment I arrived, I was amazed by the massive theme-based pandal, stunning lighting, and creative artistic presentation, which instantly created a festive and energetic atmosphere.

The idol of Goddess Durga was beautifully crafted, with intricate detailing and a majestic presence that truly stood out. I spent quite some time admiring the theme work and the overall concept, which reflected both innovation and deep-rooted tradition. The sound of dhak beats, devotional chants, and the excitement of the crowd made the entire place feel alive and immersive.

Despite the heavy crowd, the arrangements were quite organized, and I could move through the pandal comfortably. Visiting Naktala Udayan Sangha Durga Puja gave me a strong sense of cultural pride and festive joy — it was definitely one of the highlight pandals during my Durga Puja journey across Kolkata.

name: Labony Estate Durga Puja
description: Labony Estate’s Durga Puja this year has created a beautiful Rajbari (traditional Bengali mansion) themed pandal, dedicated to Lord Shiva. At the center, the idol of Maa Durga is placed, accompanied by her children—Ganesh, Lakshmi, Saraswati, and Kartikeya. This fusion of a royal architectural setting with the divine family highlights both cultural heritage and spiritual devotion, offering visitors a grand and sacred experience. The Rajbari theme adds a majestic backdrop, symbolizing reverence and opulence, while maintaining the essence of Bengal's traditional architecture.

name: EC Block Park
description: This place has a spacious playground and also a park for children. During durga pujo the pandel is formed on the play ground. In spite of the low budget EC block pujo Committee never fails to create innovative pandel in each year.
